basics.name: Junxi Yi
basics.summary: Ph.D. student in Bioengineering at the University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign. I develop ultrasound-responsive mechanophore nanocarriers and imaging-guided actuation methods to enable non-invasive, deep-tissue mechanochemical control. My work advances mechanistic understanding and system design, including bubble-nucleation and gas-vesicle integration strategies to enhance activation efficiency and localization.

education.institution: University of Science and Technology of China

work.name: Research Assistant
highlights: Led development of ultrasound-responsive mechanophore nanocarriers for controlled drug release and cancer therapy.
Designed bubble-nucleation and gas-vesicle integration strategies to enhance mechanophore activation efficiency and localization.
Mentored undergraduate students in nanoparticle synthesis and in vitro nanomedicine testing.

work.name: Research Intern
work.summary: Designed plant E3 ubiquitin ligases to degrade pathogen effectors and improve plant immunity.

work.name: Research Intern
work.summary: Developed a machine-learning algorithm for molecular potential energy surface calculations.

publications.name: Ultrasound-driven mechanophore activation in living plants
publications.publisher: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences of the United States of America

publications.name: Near-infrared-triggered nanodroplets for macrophage-targeted cancer immunotherapy
Injectable mechanophore nanoparticles for deep-tissue mechanochemical dynamic therapy
Using protein design to monomerize a bright near-infrared fluorescent protein

teaching.course: BIOE 486 – Applied Deep Learning in Biomedical Imaging
teaching.description: Developed and graded assignments; supported PyTorch-based imaging pipelines.
Led lab sections; graded reports; provided structured feedback.

teaching.course: BIOE 210 – Linear Algebra for Biomedical Data Science
teaching.description: Delivered lectures; developed MATLAB demos; hosted office hours.
